title: draba verna subsp. spathulata
intro: Draba verna subsp. spathulata, also known as Draba spathulata or Spatulate Whitlow Grass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Draba verna subsp. spathulata include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of Draba verna subsp. spathulata often involve infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to make a tea, which is then consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
